name: Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test
description: A 10-item screening tool developed by the World Health Organization (WHO) to assess alcohol consumption, drinking behaviors, and alcohol-related problems.
author: World Health Organization (WHO)
instructions: Please answer the following questions about your alcohol use. Try to answer as honestly as possible. All responses are confidential.

questions.label: How often do you have a drink containing alcohol?

skipLogic.message: No further questions needed as patient does not drink alcohol.

questions.label: How many drinks containing alcohol do you have on a typical day when you are drinking?
How often do you have six or more drinks on one occasion?
How often during the last year have you found that you were not able to stop drinking once you had started?
How often during the last year have you failed to do what was normally expected of you because of drinking?
How often during the last year have you needed a first drink in the morning to get yourself going after a heavy drinking session?
How often during the last year have you had a feeling of guilt or remorse after drinking?
How often during the last year have you been unable to remember what happened the night before because of your drinking?
Have you or someone else been injured because of your drinking?

options.label: Yes, but not in the last year

questions.label: Has a relative, friend, doctor, or other health care worker been concerned about your drinking or suggested you cut down?

options.label: Yes, but not in the last year

domains.name: Hazardous Alcohol Use
domains.description: Questions 1-3 assess the frequency and quantity of alcohol consumption

domains.name: Dependence Symptoms
domains.description: Questions 4-6 assess symptoms of alcohol dependence

domains.name: Harmful Alcohol Use
domains.description: Questions 7-10 assess harmful consequences of alcohol use

severityBands.label: Low Risk
severityBands.description: Low risk drinking or abstinence. Alcohol education may be appropriate.
severityBands.recommendation: Reinforce current drinking patterns or abstinence.

severityBands.label: Hazardous Drinking
severityBands.description: Hazardous or harmful alcohol use. Brief intervention is recommended.
severityBands.recommendation: Simple advice focusing on reduction of hazardous drinking.

severityBands.label: Harmful Drinking
severityBands.description: High level of alcohol problems. Brief counseling and continued monitoring recommended.

severityBands.label: Possible Alcohol Dependence
severityBands.description: Warrants further diagnostic evaluation for alcohol dependence. Referral to specialist recommended.
severityBands.recommendation: Referral to specialist for diagnostic evaluation and treatment.

riskRules.message: AUDIT score ≥ 20 indicates possible alcohol dependence - specialist referral recommended
Regular morning drinking indicates possible physical dependence
Alcohol-related injury in the past year - safety counseling needed
Others have expressed concern about drinking in the past year
Weekly or more frequent binge drinking (6+ drinks) detected
AUDIT-C (first 3 questions) score ≥ 5 indicates hazardous drinking in men (≥4 for women)

subscales.name: AUDIT-C (Consumption)
subscales.description: Brief 3-item screen focusing on alcohol consumption

interpretation.clinicalNotes: The AUDIT was developed by the World Health Organization (WHO) as a simple method of screening for excessive drinking and to assist in brief assessment. It helps identify persons with hazardous and harmful patterns of alcohol consumption.
interpretation.scoring: Each question is scored from 0-4, except questions 9 and 10 which are scored 0, 2, or 4. Total score range is 0-40.
interpretation.genderConsiderations: Women generally show harm at lower drinking levels than men. Some clinicians use lower cutoff scores (e.g., 7) for women.
interpretation.auditC: The first three questions (AUDIT-C) can be used as a brief screen. A score of 4+ in men or 3+ in women suggests hazardous drinking.
interpretation.limitations: Self-report measure; may be affected by social desirability bias. Cultural factors may influence responses.
references: Saunders JB, Aasland OG, Babor TF, et al. Development of the Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test (AUDIT). Addiction. 1993;88:791-804.
Babor TF, Higgins-Biddle JC, Saunders JB, Monteiro MG. AUDIT: The Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test: Guidelines for Use in Primary Care. 2nd ed. Geneva: World Health Organization; 2001.
